Freaky Footwear Is Officially For the Boys, Too
multimedia.caption: Freaky Footwear Is Officially For the Boys, Too

multimedia.alt: Are the Spring 2027 Menswear Shows Closing the Swag Gap?
multimedia.caption: Are the Spring 2027 Menswear Shows Closing the Swag Gap?

fullArticle:
From Ralph Lauren’s master class in American eclecticism and the return of skinny jeans (!) at Prada to Dior Men’s day-after party boys and Michael Rider’s expansive, energetic Celine, the spring 2027 men’s season has reached its conclusion (that is, until September, when the brands that show their collections co-ed-style rev up).

It was a season dominated by flowing suits (Armani, Soshiotsuki), smooshed little shoes (Celine, Auralee), dreamy, sherbet-y colors (Dries Van Noten, Saint Laurent), and wild swerves (Rick Owens, Junya Watanabe). Everything a man could want, really.

In this week’s episode of The Run-Through, Vogue Runway global director Nicole Phelps talks to contributing fashion critic Luke Leitch (just hours off the plane from Jacquemus’s show in Corsica) and senior fashion n...
